name: Kyruus Provider Data Management API
what_it_does: Kyruus (now part of symplr) provider data management REST API for health systems and insurers to maintain accurate provider directories, manage provider networks, and optimize patient-provider matching. Enables AI agents to manage provider profile and credentialing data management for provider directory accuracy automation, handle insurance network participation and contract data for plan provider directory automation, access patient-provider matching and care team routing for patient access optimization automation, retrieve provider availability and schedule publishing for appointment scheduling automation, manage CMS provider directory accuracy compliance for payer network management automation, handle provider affiliation and group practice management for health system provider network automation, access provider search and specialty matching for patient referral automation, retrieve provider performance and quality data for network quality management automation, manage multi-channel provider directory publishing for consumer and EHR directory automation, and integrate Kyruus with credentialing systems, EHR scheduling, and payer network management for end-to-end provider data management.
use_cases: Maintaining provider directories from health system data management agents
Matching patients to providers from care navigation automation agents
Publishing payer network directories from insurance network management agents
Optimizing provider referrals from care coordination agents
not_for: Physician credentialing and privileging (use symplr Credentialing or Verisys)
Provider contract and fee schedule negotiation (use Availity or Waystar)
Patient scheduling marketplace for consumer booking (use Zocdoc or Solv)
best_when: A health system, hospital network, or health plan wanting AI agents to automate provider directory accuracy, patient-provider matching, network management, and CMS directory compliance within Kyruus/symplr's provider data platform.
avoid_when: CMS PROVIDER DIRECTORY ACCURACY REGULATORY REQUIREMENT: Automated provider directory management via Kyruus must meet CMS Interoperability Rule provider directory accuracy requirements; automated directory updates without verification of current provider network participation status creates CMS compliance violation for health plans subject to the rule. PROVIDER DATA STALE RATE IN AUTOMATED UPDATES: Provider practice information changes frequently (address, phone, accepting new patients, insurance); automated provider directory with stale update cycle creates directory inaccuracy above CMS maximum error rate tolerance; implement automated provider data verification and outreach for high-turnover provider attributes. CREDENTIALING DATA SOURCE AUTHORITY: Kyruus provider profiles must reflect primary source verified credentialing data; automated provider profile updates must use credentialing source of record (CAQH ProView, state licensing board); automated updates from non-authoritative sources create provider profile accuracy gaps in CMS-scrutinized directory.

auth.notes: Kyruus uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. Boston, Massachusetts HQ. Founded 2010 by Graham Gardner and Gabe Khalil. Acquired by symplr in 2022. Part of symplr's healthcare operations platform (symplr also acquired HealthStream enterprise, Phynd, and other healthcare operations companies). Provider data management, patient-provider matching, and provider directory for health systems and payers. CMS directory compliance. Competes with Phynd, Definitive Healthcare, and NexHealth for provider data management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise subscription for health systems and health plans
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription based on provider count and module configuration; symplr enterprise agreements for multi-product customers
pricing.notes: Boston MA. symplr subsidiary since 2022. Annual subscription. Provider-count-based pricing. symplr multi-product discounts. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; provider and directory queries throttled; webhooks for provider update and matching events

known_agent_gotchas: SYMPLR ACQUISITION PLATFORM MIGRATION: Kyruus was acquired by symplr in 2022; symplr platform consolidation integrating Kyruus with Phynd and other symplr products may affect Kyruus API roadmap; monitor symplr developer communications for API migration and consolidation announcements before building long-term automated workflows on Kyruus-specific endpoints

CMS DIRECTORY RESPONSE TIME REQUIREMENT: CMS Interoperability Rule requires health plans to update provider directory within specified timeframes after provider change notification; automated directory update latency via Kyruus must meet CMS response time requirement; automated batch update schedule with weekly cadence may not meet CMS update timing requirement for certain change types

ACCEPTING NEW PATIENTS STATUS VERIFICATION: Provider accepting new patients status is among the most frequently changed and most inaccurate provider directory attributes; automated Kyruus directory must implement active verification outreach for accepting new patients status; automated directory with stale accepting status creates patient access failure and CMS directory accuracy violation

PROVIDER AFFILIATION TEMPORAL ACCURACY: Provider hospital and group affiliations change with contract dates; automated affiliation management via Kyruus must track affiliation start and end dates; automated directory that presents expired affiliations creates incorrect provider network membership display for payer directories

PATIENT MATCHING ALGORITHM TRAINING DATA BIAS: Kyruus patient-provider matching algorithm factors include patient preference, geographic proximity, insurance, and availability; automated matching without fairness testing may produce results with access disparities for underserved populations; implement disparate impact analysis for automated matching recommendations in health equity sensitive deployments

MULTI-SOURCE PROVIDER DATA CONFLICT RESOLUTION: Automated provider profile updates via Kyruus may receive conflicting data from multiple source systems (credentialing, EHR, billing); Kyruus data governance rules determine source priority; automated update without source authority configuration creates unresolved data conflict in provider profile
agent_readiness.security_notes: Provider data management. HIPAA, SOC2. OAuth2. US. Provider credentialing data, network participation, and patient access information.

security_score_components.security_notes: Provider data management. HIPAA, SOC2. OAuth2. US. Provider credentialing, network, and patient access data.
